stocks.name: KLA Corporation
stocks.explanation: KLA's apparent 89% drop reflects its 10-for-1 stock split that took effect on June 12, not a fundamental decline. The split was announced May 7 alongside a 21% dividend increase. KLA's previous close of ~$2,411 pre-split translates to ~$241 post-split, making the actual adjusted move modest. The split aims to improve share accessibility and liquidity for the semiconductor equipment maker.

stocks.name: Roku Inc.
stocks.explanation: Roku surged nearly 20% after Fox Corporation announced a definitive agreement to acquire the streaming platform for approximately $22 billion, or $160 per share in a mix of cash and Fox Class A stock. The deal combines Fox's Tubi service and sports/news content with Roku's 100-million-plus streaming household base, creating the third-largest U.S. television company by viewing share.

stocks.name: AST SpaceMobile
stocks.explanation: AST SpaceMobile continued its post-SpaceX IPO selloff as capital rotated out of smaller space stocks into the newly public SpaceX. Concerns about execution risk following a Bluebird 7 satellite launch anomaly added pressure. Despite the drop, the company holds $3.5 billion in cash and $1.2 billion in contracted revenue commitments from telecom partners.

stocks.name: Intuitive Machines
stocks.explanation: Intuitive Machines dropped 13% as part of a broad space-sector rotation triggered by SpaceX's record IPO on June 12. Investors shifted capital from smaller space plays into the newly public SpaceX, with Intuitive Machines hit harder than peers due to its direct competition in lunar delivery services and narrower revenue base.

stocks.name: Arm Holdings
stocks.explanation: Arm rallied 11% as the US-Iran peace deal boosted global risk appetite, amplifying momentum from Nvidia's RTX Spark superchip announcement using Arm-based architecture. Mizuho raised its price target to $500 citing agentic AI workloads, and Arm's record Q4 revenue of $1.49 billion showed 20% year-over-year growth with $2 billion in committed customer demand for its AGI CPU.

stocks.name: Rocket Lab USA
stocks.explanation: Rocket Lab fell nearly 11% in the SpaceX IPO rotation trade, as investors sold smaller space names to fund positions in the newly public SPCX. The decline was relatively contained compared to peers like Virgin Galactic (-24%). Rocket Lab will join the Nasdaq-100 on June 22 and reported strong Q1 results with $344 million revenue and a $2.2 billion backlog.

stocks.name: ZoomInfo Technologies
stocks.explanation: ZoomInfo jumped over 10% as the company's turnaround narrative gained traction ahead of its virtual investor day. Quarterly revenue of $309 million surpassed expectations with the largest sequential growth since Q2 2022. Analyst upgrades from Barclays and Citi reflect growing confidence in ZoomInfo's enterprise pivot and its Copilot product reaching $150 million in annual contract value.

stocks.name: Amkor Technology
stocks.explanation: Amkor Technology rose nearly 9% as the US-Iran peace deal lifted semiconductor stocks broadly, with particular strength in packaging and test names benefiting from sustained AI chip demand. Amkor's advanced packaging capabilities for AI accelerators position it as a key beneficiary of the ongoing data center buildout cycle.

stocks.name: Rivian Automotive
stocks.explanation: Rivian extended its rally on continued momentum from R2 SUV deliveries that began June 9, marking the company's entry into the mass-market EV segment. Volkswagen's recent purchase of a 15.9% stake and a new 5G connectivity deal with AT&T for the R2 have bolstered investor confidence. The stock is up over 30% from its May lows.

stocks.name: Seagate Technology
stocks.explanation: Seagate continued its 2026 tear — now up over 200% year-to-date — after multiple analyst upgrades from Wells Fargo, Barclays, and BofA citing AI-driven storage demand. The company announced redemption of $150.7 million in convertible notes and guided for at least 20% annual revenue growth. Recent quarterly revenue hit $3.1 billion with a 32% operating margin.

stocks.name: Reddit Inc.
stocks.explanation: Reddit dropped nearly 7%, extending a 40% year-to-date decline despite posting record Q1 revenue of $663 million (up 69% YoY). The stock remains under pressure from Meta's launch of Forum, a Reddit-clone built on Facebook Groups, and lingering concerns about Google algorithm changes that could reduce Reddit's search traffic referral pipeline.

stocks.name: Onto Innovation
stocks.explanation: Onto Innovation rose 6.6% as semiconductor equipment stocks rallied broadly on the US-Iran peace deal and continued strength in AI chip capital expenditure. As a specialist in process control for advanced packaging and lithography, Onto benefits from the multi-year upgrade cycle at TSMC, Samsung, and Intel foundries.

stocks.name: Adobe Inc.
stocks.explanation: Adobe fell 6.5% following CFO Dan Durn's abrupt departure to Marvell Technology, compounding concerns about a dual leadership vacuum as CEO Shantanu Narayen announced his own planned exit in March. Despite beating Q2 estimates with $6.62 billion in revenue (up 13% YoY), investors are wary of Adobe's shift toward a freemium AI model that could pressure near-term revenue.

stocks.name: Intel Corporation
stocks.explanation: Intel rose 6.5%, extending a 250%+ year-to-date rally fueled by its foundry turnaround. The stock is riding momentum from Google's order for 3 million+ TPUs to be manufactured at Intel fabs, a Foxconn partnership for AI rack-scale infrastructure, and Bank of America's price target raise to $135. CHIPS Act grants for Ohio and Arizona fabs have de-risked the capex outlook through 2027.

stocks.name: Western Digital
stocks.explanation: Western Digital gained 6.4% in sympathy with Seagate's continued rally as AI-driven data storage demand lifts the entire hard drive sector. Analyst upgrades across the storage space and expectations for sustained enterprise demand from hyperscaler data center builds have propelled both WDC and STX to multi-year highs in 2026.
